title: The Ukrainian family torn by war and exile behind a famous photo published by Le Monde
subhed: A few days after the Russian invasion, photojournalist Laurence Geai captured the farewell of a Ukrainian family at Odesa train station. Four years later, the mother welcomed Le Monde to Cologne, Germany, where she lives as a refugee with her son and her mother, far from her husband, since enlisted in the army.
byline: By Faustine Vincent (Cologne (Germany), special correspondent) and Laurence Geai (photographer)

title: The Syrian oil sector's year zero
subhed: By driving the Kurds out of northeastern Syria, the central government has regained control over all of the country's oil resources. However, given the poor condition of the wells, damaged by fighting and chaotic extraction, it will take several years before production returns to its pre-war level.

subhed: From creating campaign videos to writing speeches, artificial intelligence has become an effective tool for candidates. However, it also raises ethical questions, prompting some elected officials to call for regulation.

title: Documents show Epstein's attempts at connecting with Sarkozy's circle
subhed: Nicolas Princen, an adviser to Nicolas Sarkozy at the Elysée Palace, met the sex offender in his Paris apartment in 2011. The meeting, which 'had no follow-up,' according to Princen, illustrates Epstein's attempts to approach the French presidency under Sarkozy.

title: Serge Gainsbourg's forgotten Ukrainian roots
subhed: Serge Gainsbourg described himself as a Russian Jew. More precisely, his musician parents fled Tsarist Ukraine before emigrating to Paris in 1921, to escape from pogroms and the Bolsheviks. In Ukraine, where four years of war have fueled an obsession with uncovering traces of the past, some have been trying to rediscover the singer's roots.
